column2: I honestly never imagined silence could become so unbearably loud that it would keep me awake at night even after everyone else had already fallen asleep!
Why does this room still feel like you are here when you already walked away hours ago?
Every single corner reminds me of you somehow, and I hate how impossible it feels to escape those memories!
I keep replaying our last conversation in my head like some broken recording that refuses to stop no matter how badly I want peace.
Maybe if I had answered differently, things would have turned out better between us.
Or maybe we were already falling apart long before either of us was brave enough to admit it.
Do you ever think about that too?
Because I do, constantly!
I hate how much I still care about someone who made me question my own worth over and over again.
Actually, no, hate is the wrong word.
What I really hate is the fact that you still matter to me after everything that happened between us!
You always knew exactly what to say whenever you wanted to get close to me emotionally.
How did you learn me so well?
Seriously, how?
It felt like you memorized every weakness, every fear, and every hidden insecurity I spent years trying to bury inside myself.
And the worst part is that I let you.
I trusted you completely without even realizing how dangerous that would become later on.
God, when I think about it now, I sounded so hopeful back then!
I told you things I had never spoken out loud before, things that honestly terrified me to admit even to myself.
Did you understand how important that trust was to me?
Or was I just another temporary person passing through your life for a little while?
Sometimes I wonder if I ever meant as much to you as you meant to me.
You looked so calm during our last conversation, and meanwhile I was barely holding myself together inside.
How do people walk away so easily without completely breaking apart afterward?!
Because my hands still shake whenever I think too deeply about everything we lost.
I kept telling myself I was only overthinking things.
Then I saw the way you looked at her.
That tiny smile.
That softer voice.
That familiar warmth you used to reserve for me.
You probably thought nobody noticed, but I noticed immediately!
I always noticed everything about you, remember?
Maybe that was my biggest mistake.
Maybe loving someone deeply just means giving them the power to destroy you slowly.
I tried acting mature about the whole thing instead of becoming emotional in front of everyone.
I smiled.
I laughed.
I pretended I was fine!
But every fake smile felt heavier than the last one until even breathing started feeling exhausting.
I'm tired, not physically tired, but emotionally exhausted in a way sleep cannot fix anymore.
It feels like carrying invisible weight inside my chest every single day.
Some mornings I wake up and immediately wish I could skip the entire day altogether.
But don't misunderstand me.
I'm still trying!
Even now, I'm still trying to rebuild myself piece by piece.
Do you know how exhausting it is to start over emotionally after giving someone your entire heart?
Do you know what it feels like to stop yourself from checking your phone every five minutes hoping for a message that never arrives?
It's humiliating!
And somehow addictive at the same time.
I miss you most during random little moments that shouldn't even matter this much.
Whenever something funny happens, my first instinct is still to turn toward you before reality hits me again.
Isn't that pathetic?
My brain still reaches for someone who is no longer here.
Sometimes I wonder if I ever cross your mind unexpectedly during ordinary moments.
Like while you're waiting in traffic.
Or standing in line at some random store.
Or lying awake at night unable to sleep.
Do you ever miss me too?
Or was I only important while I was convenient for you?
Because to me, you felt permanent.
You felt like home.
And maybe that's why losing you feels like losing part of myself too.
I built entire futures around small promises you probably forgot seconds after saying them.
I remembered everything about you!
Your favorite drink order.
The songs you always skipped halfway through.
The way your voice changed whenever you were exhausted or upset.
I paid attention to every little detail because loving you became automatic for me.
People always describe deep love as beautiful and romantic, but nobody talks about how terrifying it becomes when things fall apart.
Sometimes loving someone too much feels less like flying and more like drowning slowly.
And honestly?
If you walked through that door right now, I don't even know what I would do anymore.
Part of me would want to scream at you for everything you put me through!
Another part of me would probably collapse into your arms without thinking twice.
That's what scares me the most.
I want to be stronger than this.
I want to hear your name without feeling my chest tighten painfully.
I want to stop turning old memories into evidence while trying to figure out whether you ever truly loved me at all.
But healing isn't simple, is it?
Some days I feel completely fine.
Then suddenly one song ruins everything again!
Today is one of those days.
I accidentally wore the sweater you once said looked beautiful on me, and somehow that tiny detail destroyed my mood instantly.
Why am I still holding onto things connected to you?!
Maybe part of me still wants to feel loved the way you once made me feel.
Or maybe I just miss the version of myself that existed before everything became complicated.
She seemed happier.
Lighter.
Less afraid of abandonment and disappointment.
I miss her almost as much as I miss you.
But I know she's not gone forever.
She's buried underneath heartbreak, anxiety, sleepless nights, and memories that still refuse to leave me alone.
And one day, maybe not today and maybe not soon, I know I'll finally stop measuring my worth based on who decided to stay.
One day your name won't hurt anymore.
One day these memories will feel distant instead of suffocating.
One day I'll tell this story without my voice shaking halfway through it.
But today?
Today it still hurts.
